subtasks.description: Query spend data for current month and prior 3 months (rolling average), calculate variance metrics, and identify anomalies. Return current month spend, 3-month average, variance percentage, and raw anomaly flags.

Reconcile spend anomalies from SQL results and classify each anomaly into three tiers (normal_variance, concerning, critical) based on variance magnitude, spend velocity, and contextual thresholds. Return classified anomalies with reasoning.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) sql_gen (mid tier) for multi-month spend aggregation with rolling 3-month average calculation and variance computation—this involves time-windowed aggregation across 4+ months of data, moderate complexity; (2) cross_recon (mid tier) for classification logic, as it reconciles the SQL anomaly signals against threshold policies and assigns risk categories. Both tiers are mid because the spend query requires joins/window functions but not extreme complexity, and the classification applies clear multi-threshold logic without conflicting policies. No policy_action needed because classification is part of the analytical workflow, not governance approval.

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "WITH monthly_spend AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS spend_month,
    SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend
  FROM consumption_facts
  WHERE event_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)
),
current_month AS (
  SELECT
    spend_month,
    total_spend AS current_spend
  FROM monthly_spend
  WHERE spend_month = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
),
prior_3_months AS (
  SELECT
    AVG(total_spend) AS avg_3mo_spend,
    STDDEV(total_spend) AS stddev_3mo_spend
  FROM monthly_spend
  WHERE spend_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
    AND spend_month < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
),
per_publisher_current AS (
  SELECT
    publisher,
    SUM(amount_usd) AS current_month_spend
  FROM consumption_facts
  WHERE event_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
    AND event_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  GROUP BY publisher
),
per_publisher_prior AS (
  SELECT
    publisher,
    AVG(monthly_spend) AS avg_3mo_spend,
    STDDEV(monthly_spend) AS stddev_3mo_spend
  FROM (
    SELECT
      publisher,
      DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS spend_month,
      SUM(amount_usd) AS monthly_spend
    FROM consumption_facts
    WHERE event_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
      AND event_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
    GROUP BY publisher, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)
  ) sub
  GROUP BY publisher
),
publisher_anomalies AS (
  SELECT
    c.publisher,
    c.current_month_spend,
    COALESCE(p.avg_3mo_spend, 0) AS avg_3mo_spend,
    COALESCE(p.stddev_3mo_spend, 0) AS stddev_3mo_spend,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(p.avg_3mo_spend, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE ROUND(
        ((c.current_month_spend - p.avg_3mo_spend) / p.avg_3mo_spend) * 100, 2
      )
    END AS variance_pct,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(p.avg_3mo_spend, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE ROUND(
        ABS(c.current_month_spend -

sql_value.note: SQL query was truncated mid-execution before publisher_anomalies CTE could be completed and results returned

reconciled.reasoning: SQL output was truncated before anomaly results could be retrieved. No numeric spend data is available from either MongoDB or SQL. Cannot compute variance_pct, z-scores, or spend velocity. All downstream classification is blocked. Recommend re-running the query to retrieve complete results.
DEFINITION (applied when data is available): variance_pct between -15% and +15% AND z-score < 1.5. Spend is within expected seasonal and operational bounds. No action required.

DEFINITION (applied when data is available): variance_pct between +15% and +40% OR z-score between 1.5 and 2.5 OR spend velocity (day-over-day acceleration) > 10% week-over-week. Warrants review and budget owner notification.

DEFINITION (applied when data is available): variance_pct > +40% OR z-score > 2.5 OR publisher has zero prior 3-month spend baseline (new unconstrained spend detected) OR stddev = 0 with any positive variance. Requires immediate escalation and possible spend freeze.
